{"id":"autoimprove-i2l.1","title":"Bead 1: Project Scaffolding","description":"Set up Python project structure, CLI entrypoint, config schema, and all __init__.py stubs.

FILES TO CREATE:
- autoimprove.sh (shell entrypoint wrapper)
- pyproject.toml (Python project: pydantic, click, rich, pyyaml, pytest, ruff)
- config.yaml (default runtime config template)
- program.md (template with placeholder content)
- src/__init__.py, src/cli.py, src/config.py, src/types.py
- src/orchestrator.py, src/run_context.py, src/git_ops.py, src/agent_bridge.py, src/preflight.py, src/policy.py (all stubs)
- src/eval/__init__.py, engine.py, baseline.py, criteria.py, llm_judge.py, search_memory.py (all stubs)
- src/plugins/__init__.py, base.py, code_plugin.py, workflow_plugin.py, document_plugin.py, registry.py (all stubs)
- src/reporting/__init__.py, experiment_log.py, summary.py, terminal.py (all stubs)
- profiles/code.md, profiles/workflow.md, profiles/document.md

KEY DETAILS:
- src/config.py: Pydantic BaseModel with all config fields (agent_command, time_budget_minutes, target_paths, etc.)
- src/types.py: Decision enum, ConfidenceProfile enum, RunStatus enum, ExperimentOutcome, Diff, GateResult, SoftEvalResult dataclasses
- src/cli.py: click CLI with commands: run, merge, discard, status (all stubbed except arg parsing)
- All stubs must have class/function signatures with docstrings and raise NotImplementedError

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- uv sync succeeds
- uv run python -m src.cli --help prints help
- Config loads and validates from config.yaml
- All stub files importable with no errors
- autoimprove.sh delegates to Python correctly

{"id":"autoimprove-i2l.2","title":"Bead 2: Git Operations","description":"Implement all git operations: worktree management, commits, tags, reverts, diff extraction, run ID generation.

FILES TO EDIT:
- src/git_ops.py (replace stub with full implementation)

KEY FUNCTIONS (15 total):
- generate_run_id() -> str (format: YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS-<6char_hash>)
- ensure_git_repo(path) -> bool
- is_repo_clean(path) -> bool
- get_current_branch(path) -> str
- get_head_sha(path) -> str
- create_worktree(repo_path, worktree_path, branch_name) -> str
- remove_worktree(repo_path, worktree_path) -> None
- commit(worktree_path, message, files=None) -> str (returns SHA)
- tag(worktree_path, tag_name) -> None
- revert_to_commit(worktree_path, commit_sha) -> None
- get_diff(worktree_path, from_ref, to_ref='HEAD') -> Diff
- get_diff_staged(worktree_path) -> Diff
- get_last_commit_sha(worktree_path) -> str
- merge_branch_to(repo_path, source_branch, target_branch) -> bool
- delete_branch(repo_path, branch_name) -> None

ERROR HANDLING:
- Custom GitError(Exception) with command, stderr, returncode
- _run_git(args, cwd) helper for all subprocess calls
- All via subprocess.run with capture_output=True, text=True

CONVENTIONS:
- Worktree branch: autoimprove/<run_id>
- Tags: autoimprove/<run_id>/<label> (baseline, iter_N, final)
- All git commands specify cwd explicitly

DEPENDS ON: Nothing (parallel with Bead 1)

{"id":"autoimprove-i2l.3","title":"Bead 3: Run Context","description":"Manage run lifecycle: directory structure, state tracking, accepted state, budget consumption.

FILES TO EDIT:
- src/run_context.py (replace stub with full implementation)

CLASS: RunContext
- Constructor: __init__(config: Config, repo_path: str) — generates run_id, sets up paths
- Fields: run_id, repo_path, config, status, current_iteration, start_time, accepted_state_sha, baseline_sha, total_accepts, total_rejects, consecutive_rejections, criteria_version
- Paths: run_dir, worktree_path, config_path, baseline_path, accepted_state_path, experiment_log_path, search_memory_path, criteria_dir, proposals_dir, summary_path

METHODS (11):
- initialize() — creates dir structure + worktree + symlink .autoimprove/latest
- set_baseline(sha) — records baseline SHA, tags it
- record_accept(sha) — updates accepted_state_sha, increments accepts, resets consecutive_rejections, tags iter_N
- record_reject() — increments rejects + consecutive_rejections, reverts worktree to accepted_state_sha
- elapsed_minutes() -> float
- budget_remaining_minutes() -> float
- is_budget_exhausted() -> bool
- save_state() — persists to accepted_state.json
- load_state(cls, run_dir) — classmethod, reconstructs from disk
- finalize() — sets COMPLETED, tags final
- cleanup() — removes worktree

DIR STRUCTURE CREATED:
.autoimprove/runs/<run_id>/{worktree/, config.yaml, criteria/, proposals/, baseline.json, accepted_state.json, experiment_log.json, search_memory.json, summary.md}
.autoimprove/latest -> runs/<run_id> (symlink)

DEPENDS ON: Bead 1 (config.py, types.py), Bead 2 (git_ops.py)

{"id":"autoimprove-i2l.4","title":"Bead 4: Preflight","description":"Validate entire environment before starting a run. Fail fast with clear, actionable errors.

FILES TO EDIT:
- src/preflight.py (replace stub with full implementation)

DATACLASSES:
- PreflightResult: passed, checks, errors, warnings
- PreflightCheck: name, passed, message, fatal

MAIN FUNCTION: run_preflight(config, repo_path) -> PreflightResult
- Runs ALL checks (no short-circuit), reports all failures at once

CHECKS (11 total):
1. check_git_repo (fatal) — is this a git repo?
2. check_git_clean (fatal) — no uncommitted changes?
3. check_python_version (fatal) — Python >= 3.10?
4. check_agent_command (fatal) — agent binary in PATH?
5. check_target_paths (fatal) — all target paths exist and non-empty?
6. check_exclude_paths_not_overlapping (warning) — target not also excluded?
7. check_config_valid (fatal) — config passes validation?
8. check_disk_space (warning) — >1GB available?
9. check_program_md_exists (warning) — program.md present?
10. check_secret_scan (warning) — no secrets in target paths?
11. check_worktree_path_available (fatal) — can create .autoimprove/runs/?

ALSO: print_preflight_report(result) using rich — green ✓, red ✗, yellow ⚠

DEPENDS ON: Bead 1 (config.py), Bead 2 (git_ops.py), Bead 3 (run_context.py)

{"id":"autoimprove-i2l.7","title":"Bead 7: Policy Enforcement","description":"Guardrail layer that validates every candidate diff BEFORE evaluation. Safety net for immediate rejection.

FILES TO EDIT:
- src/policy.py (replace stub)

DATACLASSES:
- PolicyViolation: rule, severity (fatal/warning), file, line, message
- PolicyResult: passed, violations list, fatal_count, warning_count

MAIN FUNCTION: check_policy(diff, config, plugin_guardrails=None) -> PolicyResult
- Runs ALL checks (no short-circuit)

CHECKS (7):
1. check_diff_size (fatal) — lines changed > max_diff_lines?
2. check_protected_paths (fatal) — files match protected_paths or plugin patterns? (fnmatch)
3. check_forbidden_extensions (fatal) — file extensions in forbidden list?
4. check_secret_patterns (fatal) — added lines match secret patterns? (AWS keys, GitHub tokens, OpenAI keys, private keys)
5. check_dependency_changes (fatal if allow_dependency_changes=False) — package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, etc.
6. check_excluded_paths (fatal) — changed files in exclude_paths?
7. check_empty_diff (fatal) — no changes at all?

NOTES:
- Secret scan only checks added lines (+ prefix in diff), not removed
- Skip +++ header lines in diff parsing
- Purely deterministic, no LLM dependency
- Runs BEFORE hard gates (cheaper, catches obvious problems)

DEPENDS ON: Bead 1 (config.py, types.py), Bead 2 (git_ops.py)

{"id":"autoimprove-i2l.10","title":"Bead 10: Criteria Management","description":"Versioned evaluation criteria — the rubric defining 'better'. Immutable within a run (v1 policy).

FILES TO EDIT:
- src/eval/criteria.py (replace stub)

DATACLASSES:
- CriteriaItem: name, description, weight (0-1), is_hard_gate, metric_type (deterministic|judgment)
- CriteriaVersion: version int, created_at, items list, plugin_name, notes
- CriteriaProposal: proposed_at, iteration, changes list, rationale, status (always 'pending' in v1)

CLASS: CriteriaManager
- __init__(criteria_dir: Path)

METHODS (13):
- create_initial(items, plugin_name, notes) -> CriteriaVersion — validates weights sum to ~1.0, saves v1
- get_current() -> CriteriaVersion — highest version (always v1 in v1)
- get_version(version) -> CriteriaVersion
- record_proposal(iteration, changes, rationale) -> CriteriaProposal — saves but does NOT apply
- get_proposals() -> list[CriteriaProposal]
- get_weights_dict() -> dict[str, float]
- get_hard_gates() -> list[str]
- to_rubric_items() -> list[JudgeRubricItem]
- save(version) -> None (pretty-printed JSON)
- load_all(cls, criteria_dir) -> CriteriaManager (classmethod)

DEFAULT TEMPLATES (class methods):
- default_code_criteria(): test_pass_rate(gate), build_success(gate), lint_score(0.2), complexity(0.2), readability(0.25), maintainability(0.2), error_handling(0.15)
- default_document_criteria(): parseable(gate), clarity(0.3), completeness(0.25), structure(0.2), audience_fit(0.15), actionability(0.1)
- default_workflow_criteria(): schema_valid(gate), error_handling(0.25), efficiency(0.2), reliability(0.25), readability(0.15), security(0.15)

V1 POLICY: Criteria immutable within a run. Proposals captured for next run.

DEPENDS ON: Bead 1 (config.py, types.py)

Implemented: SemanticDiff type in types.py, semantic_diff() base method in plugin, wired into eval engine (uses semantic diff text for judge when available), wired into reviewer (accepts semantic_diff_text param), wired into multi_orchestrator (_get_semantic_diff helper queries plugin for changed files)

Implemented: IterationStrategy enum (AUTO/INTERACTIVE/BATCH/PREVIEW) in types.py, iteration_strategy() base method in plugin (derives from confidence_profile), wired into multi_orchestrator decision block — AUTO auto-accepts, INTERACTIVE/PREVIEW prompts user per change with accept/reject/quit flow

Implemented: get_calibration_lessons() in ProjectMemory (distills false positives/negatives into patterns, threshold delta, judge_context, analyst_context). Wired into: eval engine (threshold adjustment via calibration_threshold_delta param), LLM judge (judge_context appended to eval anchors), analyst agent (calibration_context param injected into prompt), both orchestrators (single-agent and multi-agent) pass calibration lessons through the full pipeline

Implemented AgentPlugin in src/plugins/agent_plugin.py — full EvaluatorPlugin implementation with: discovery of system prompts, tool defs, agent configs; guardrail detection/preservation; behavioral evaluation categories (prompt_clarity, tool_quality, guardrails, efficiency, coherence); PREVIEW iteration strategy; registered in registry; agent section added to eval_anchors.yaml

Implemented --dry-run and --preview CLI flags. Dry-run: runs grounding + analyst, shows backlog and top proposals without committing. Preview: collects up to 5 proposals with scores, presents ranked list at end. Both respect time budget. Wired through cli.py → orchestrator.py → multi_orchestrator.py
